titlestmt.titleproper: Publications on the costs of medical care
Costs of medical care, publications on
titlestmt.author: Katherine Isham

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 22:09

did.unittitle: Publications on the costs of medical care

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes pamphlets and articles regarding the costs of medical care for medical practitioners, medical institutions, and patients, 1928-1980 (bulk of materials are from 1928-1950). Topics include contract medical practice, medical economics, medical service plans, national medical insurance programs, personal medical insurance plans, physician incomes, and public health expenditures. Series I. includes materials produced by the American Medical Association; Series II. includes materials created by national and state government bureaus, national committees related to medical care, medical companies, and individual authors. Most materials are related to health care in the United States, a small number are related to the National Health Service of Great Britain.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was created by the Medical Historical Library. Provenance of individual items in the collection is unknown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in two series: Series I. American Medical Association, circa 1931-1980. and Series II. Other, 1928-1964. Series I. is further arranged by date. Series II. is further arranged by author.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Publications on the costs of medical care. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes pamphlets and articles regarding the costs of medical care for medical practitioners, medical institutions, and patients, 1928-1980 (bulk of materials are from 1928-1950). Topics include contract medical practice, medical economics, medical service plans, national medical insurance programs, personal medical insurance plans, physician incomes, and public health expenditures. Most materials are related to health care in the United States, a small number are related to the National Health Service of Great Britain.
Series I. American Medical Association, circa 1931-1980 includes materials produced by the American Medical Association.
Series II. Other, 1928-1964, includes materials created by national and state government bureaus, national committees related to medical care, medical companies, and individual authors.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: American Medical Association
American Medical Association commentaries, editorials, and speeches
Leland, R.G., and American Medical Association, Income from medical practice . Chicago: circa 1931.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Contract practice . Chicago: circa 1932.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Some phases of contract practice . Chicago: circa 1932.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, An introduction to medical economics . Chicago: 1933.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Group hospitalization contracts are insurance contracts . Chicago: circa 1933.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Group practice . Chicago: circa 1933.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Prepayment plans for hospital care . Chicago: circa 1933.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, A critical analysis of sickness insurance . Chicago: 1934.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Health insurance in England and medical society plans in the United States . Chicago: 1934.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Sickness insurance not the remedy . Chicago: 1934.
Leland, R.G., American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics. Distribution of physicians in the United States . Chicago: 1935.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Medical service plans . Chicago: circa 1935.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Some defects in insurance propaganda . Chicago: circa 1935.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Economics and the ethics of medicine . Chicago: 1936.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Group medical practice . Chicago: 1940.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Organized payments for medical services . Chicago: 1941.
American Medical Association, Medical care prepayment plan development . Council on Medical Service Special Brochure, no. 3. Chicago: 1947.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Four recent articles on medical economics . Chicago: 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "A medical service area map of the United States," "The ages of physicians in the United Staes, 1940 and 1948," "Medical care prices vs. cost of living," and "The leading causes of death among physicians."

did.unittitle: Dickinson, Frank G., American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, Analysis of a statement on medical care. Bulletin 77. Chicago: 1950.
Dickinson, Frank G., Everett L. Welker, American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, Infant deaths and stillbirths in leading nations . Bulletin 73. Chicago: 1950.

Dickinson, Frank G., American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, Latest data on personal consumer expenditures for medical care . Bureau of Medical Economic Research Miscellaneous Publication M-37. Chicago: 1950.
Dickinson, Frank G., American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, Medical care versus cost of living. Bulletin 76. Chicago: 1950.
Dickinson, Frank G., American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, list of miscellaneous bureau publications. Chicago: 1950.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economic Research, The story in charts of the economic position of medical care, 1929-1953 . Bulletin 99A. Chicago: 1955.
American Medical Association, Legislative Department, Federal medical-health appropriations for fiscal 1963 . circa 1963.
American Medical Association, The ? cost of medical care . Chicago: 1980.
American Medical Association, Bureau of Medical Economics, Care of the indigent sick . Chicago: undated.
American Medical Association, New forms of medical practice . Undated.
American Medical Association, Sickness insurance and sickness costs . Chicago: undated
Other sources
American Hospital Association, Council on Community Relations and Administrative Practices, Essentials on an acceptable plan of group hospitalization . Undated
American Public Health Association, Subcommittee on Medical Care, The quality of medical care in a national health program . 1949.
Armstrong, Donald B., A study of sickness cost and private medical practice . May 13, 1932.
Armstrong, Donald. B., Louis I. Dublin, Elizabeth J. Steele, What medical care costs the average family . The Modern Hospital vol. XLI, no. 5. November, 1933.
Brackenbury, Sir Henry B., Health insurance in England . circa 1934.
Bulletin of the American College of Surgeons vol. XIII, no. 4. Chicago: American College of Surgeons, December, 1929.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue title "Papers read at the twelfth annual hospital conference, Chicago, October 14-18." Includes multiple articles on medical economics.

did.unittitle: Committee for the Study of Group Medical Practice, Group medical practice: tentative statement of principles and procedures . Committee on Research in Medical Economics, March, 1940.
Committee on the Costs of Medical Care, Medical care for the American people . Chicago: The University of Chicago Press, undated.
Committee on Research in Medical Economics, Restrictions on free enterprise in medicine . United States of America: April, 1949
Connecticut Plan for Hospital Care Inc., And no red tape . Undated.
Connecticut State Department of Health, Your town: where does it stand on public health expenditures 1951 . circa 1952.
Connecticut State Department of Health, Is your community in balance for health protection? Undated
Davis, Michael M., The American approach to health insurance . The Milbank Memorial Fund Quarterly. circa 1934.
"The Doctors Look at Medicine." The Nation . 1931.
Federal Security Agency, Social Security Board, Bureau of Research and Statistics, Prepayment medical care organizations . circa 1944.
Fitzgerald, Hilde and Aneurin Bevan, A guide to the National Health Service Act of 1946 . London: The Labour Party, circa 1946.
Folks, Homer, The distribution of the costs of sickness in the United States . circa 1928.
Foundation for Management Research, Doctor's offices in hospital-financed buildings . Chicago: 1963.
Frankel, Lee K. and Metropolitan Life Insurance Company, Costs of medical care . New York: Metropolitan Life Insurance Company Press, circa 1929.
Gruenberg, Benjamin C., Motivation in health education . American Public Health Association, circa 1933.
Hilborn, James D., The essentials of an adequate health program . circa 1950.
Hospital Service Fund, Inc., application card. Undated.
Lewinski Corin, E.H. and Michael M. Davis, "State medicine" abroad and its relation to hospitals . Chicago: Julius Rosenwald Fund, circa 1931.
Manson, Julius, League for Industrial Democracy, Inc., The British health service . New York: Astoria Press, 1951.
Maslow, Harold, The intelligent consumer's guide to hospital and medical plans . League for Industrial Democracy pamphlet series. April, 1942.
Medical Group Management Association, The medical group manager . 1964.
Miller, James Alexander, Report of round-table on medical care . circa 1934.
New York University Alumni Bulletin vol. X, no. 4. Summer, 1946.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article on fundraising for New York University's new medical center.

did.unittitle: Public Affairs Committee, Doctors, dollars, and diseases . Public Affairs Pamplets no. 10. 1937
Rappleye, Willard C., The national health service of Great Britain . New York: Josiah Macy, Jr. Foundation, 1949.
Rappleye, Willard C., Labor, management, and medicine . 1962.
Research Council for Economic Security, State cash sickness plan . Publication no. 74. October, 1949.
Reynolds, Ralph Arthur, "How necessary is illness?" Atlantic Monthly . The Atlantic Monthly Company, June 1930.
Rorem, C. Rufus, American Hospital Association, Hospital care in the family budget . Circa 1934.
Rorem, C. Rufus, Recent developments in group hospitalization . Chicago: R.R. Donnelley & Sons Co., circa 1936.
Rorty, James, National Organ of the Council for Social Action, Is health the public's business? Social Action 3, no. 6. New York: The Pilgrim Press, March 15, 1937.
Julius Rosenwald Fund, A picture-book about the costs of medical care . Chicago: circa 1932.
Sharp and Dohme, Voluntary health plan . Philadelphia: May, 1950.
Warbasse, James Peter, Cooperative Medicine . New York: The Cooperative League, circa 1939.
Winthrop-Steams Inc., Freedom of medical practice . 1950.
